reasoning: The informal statement asks us to prove that the operation a⋆b = a−b on ℤ is not commutative. "Not commutative" is the negation of "∀ a b, a⋆b = b⋆a", which is classically equivalent to "∃ a b, a⋆b ≠ b⋆a". The Lean statement `∃ a b : ℤ, a - b ≠ b - a` expresses exactly this: there exist integers a and b for which the operation fails to commute. The types (ℤ) and the operation (subtraction) match the informal statement. Hence the formalization is strictly faithful.
